title: AgentSpec TypeScript type uses top-level prompt fields while YAML parser reads nested prompt.system/prompt.task — silent data loss across the shape divergence
description: The TypeScript AgentSpec interface at packages/pi-jit-agents/src/types.ts lines 21-37 declares top-level fields: `systemPrompt?: string`, `systemPromptTemplate?: string`, `taskPrompt?: string`, `taskPromptTemplate?: string`. The YAML parser at packages/pi-jit-agents/src/agent-spec.ts lines 178-179 reads `spec.prompt?.system` and `spec.prompt?.task` (nested under a `prompt` object). The two shapes do not agree. An author serializing a spec object that matches the TypeScript type to YAML produces `systemPrompt: ...` / `taskPrompt: ...` at the top level — and parseAgentYaml ignores both keys because it looks under `prompt.*`. The author receives no warning; the parsed spec has empty prompt fields; compileAgent reports 'no prompt content produced'.

evidence.reference: FB-004 'Two competing AgentSpec shapes: TypeScript type vs YAML input'; actual_behavior: writing {systemPrompt, taskPrompt} produces YAML with top-level keys; parseAgentYaml reads spec.prompt?.system and spec.prompt?.task — both undefined; no prompt content is produced

Investigation report FB-004 section confirms both surfaces verbatim; verdict 'Schema (canonical format definition)' — pick one shape (Option A: nested prompt; Option B: top-level fields) and enforce across TypeScript type + YAML schema + YAML parser + author-agent-spec serializer
AgentSpec interface declaring top-level systemPrompt / systemPromptTemplate / taskPrompt / taskPromptTemplate
parseAgentYaml reads spec.prompt?.system and spec.prompt?.task — nested under a prompt object, not top-level

impact: Author-side tooling that constructs AgentSpec objects from the TypeScript type and serializes them to YAML produces specs that the parser cannot read. The divergence is structural — not a transient code drift — and persists across every author surface that consumes the TypeScript type as the authoring contract. Without canonical-shape enforcement, the divergence will recur whenever new prompt-shape fields are added to either side.
proposed_resolution: Schema decision — pick ONE canonical shape: Option A (nested `prompt: { system?: string, task?: string }`) or Option B (top-level `systemPrompt: string, taskPrompt: string`) per the investigation-report verdict. After decision: update TypeScript type at packages/pi-jit-agents/src/types.ts to match the chosen shape; update YAML parser at packages/pi-jit-agents/src/agent-spec.ts to read the chosen shape; author or extend a JSON schema (AgentSpec.schema.json) declaring the canonical shape so the divergence cannot recur silently. Bundled author-agent-spec serializer must write the same shape it parses.
